mnemonics: SPARE V: A subgraph is a Selection of Parts from A Reference Entity, Verified to be Valid.
VESSEL: A subgraph is a Valid Excerpt from a Set, Still Entirely Logical.
keyPoints: A subgraph is formed from a subset of the vertices and a subset of the edges of the original graph.
Every edge in the subgraph must have both its endpoints included in the subgraph's vertex set.
The vertex set of a subgraph cannot be empty, though an edgeless graph (just vertices) is a valid subgraph.
commonMistakes: Assuming any selection of vertices and edges from G is automatically a subgraph (edges must be validated).
Forgetting that an edge cannot exist in the subgraph without its endpoints also being present.
